description_long: This is a freestanding stone marker with a roughly trapezoidal shape, featuring a textured, weathered surface. The front face displays a blue square plaque with a yellow scallop shell symbol and a yellow arrow pointing to the right, both in relief. The shell and arrow are iconic symbols of the Camino de Santiago, used to guide pilgrims along the route. The remaining sides of the marker are plain, with the same rough stone texture and no additional markings or symbols. The top surface is flat and slightly uneven, consistent with carved stone. This type of marker is typically found along walking paths or roadsides, serving as a durable and highly visible directional guide.
description: A weathered stone marker with a blue and yellow Camino de Santiago shell and right-pointing arrow on its front face, used for wayfinding.
description_view_0: The front view shows the blue plaque with a yellow scallop shell and a right-pointing yellow arrow, both in relief.
description_view_1: The left side is plain, showing only the rough, textured stone surface with no markings.
description_view_2: The back view reveals a broad, unadorned stone surface with a coarse texture.
description_view_3: The right side is also plain, matching the left with a rough stone finish and no symbols.
attribute_location_context: roadside, walking trail, or urban park along the Camino de Santiago
